subtitles.text:
♪ Let's go walking
You and me ♪

♪ Ready, set
Come on, let's go ♪

♪ Over the hill
Across the field ♪
♪ And down the bumpy gravel road ♪

♪ Let's go walking
You and me ♪

♪ Ready, set
Come on, let's go ♪
♪ The foxes and the badgers, too ♪
♪ The deep and wonderful woods all day ♪
♪ Look at all my many friends ♪
♪ Look at all my many friends ♪

- Hey Dad, want some caramel?
- Thanks. How you doing back there?

Whoops. I thought
that was a policeman.

Hello there. Are your parents around?
We're your new neighbors.

Hello! Hi, we're the Kusakabes!
We're moving in down the street.

Why don't you and your
family stop by sometime?
MAN: Okay, Sure. Good to meet you.

revisions.text: MAN: Okay, sure. Good to meet you.
revisions.note: Changed 'Sure' to lowercase 'sure' after the comma for correct punctuation.

subtitles.text:
There's a fish! Oh, look!
There's another one!
So, how do you like the new place?
Look at this. A tunnel of trees.

- It looks like it could be haunted.
- What, haunted?

- Wow! It's huge, isn't it?
- Ah-choo!
Don't forget to take your shoes off, girls.

Out of the way, Mei.
I can't close the door with you there.
Dad, acorns are falling from the ceiling.

I think rats eat acorns.
Maybe they're rats.
Mister, where do you want your radio?

Oh, right, the radio.
In here's good, I guess.

- Satsuki, open the back of the house?
- Okay.

- Can you find the back door?
- Of course!
Dad, something's running around in here.

- More squirrels?
- I don't think so.

They're not bugs or mice, either.
They were black, and they were all over the place.
It was probably just some soot gremlins.

What do you mean, “gremlins”?
Like the ones in my book?

but ghosts don't usually like
to come out on nice days like this.

Normally, you can't see soot gremlins,
but every once in a while,

when you go from a bright place to
a dark one, you can catch a glimpse of them.

BOTH: Come out, come out,
wherever you are!

Hey, I need your help.
It looks like somebody's hidden
the stairs to the top floor of the house.

Can you find the stairs and open
all the windows up there?
Come out, come out, wherever you are!
We know you're in here, soot gremlins.

Dad, come here! There's definitely
something weird in this house!

I've always wanted to live in a haunted
house ever since I was a little boy.

This is our neighbor.
She takes care of this house.

She said you can call her
“Granny” if you'd like.

I'm Satsuki, and that's Mei.
Nice to meet you.
Well, it's nice to meet you, too.
I'm sorry I didn't have time
to clean up more before you got here.

GRANNY: We've been busy
in the rice fields,

but I did manage to keep the place
well-dusted, as you can see.

Mei, your hands are filthy.
What on earth did you do?
I caught a soot gremlin, but he got away.
You've got soot sprites in your house.

Soot sprites? The black puff balls?
About this big?

They scurry everywhere
when you enter a room?
They live in old, empty houses

and run all over the place,
covering everything with dirt.

I used to be able to see them
when I was your age.

So you've seen them, too.
That's very interesting.
So they're a kind of ghost, then?

Don't worry, dear.
They're nothing to be afraid of.

If they decide you're nice people,
they won't harm you,
and after a while, they'll just go away.
I bet they're up in the rafters right now,

having a talk about whether
or not they should move out.

- Hear that, Mei? They're gonna move out.
- I don't want them to leave.

What if a huge one
came chasing after you?
Right. Then you can go to the bathroom
by yourself in the middle of the night.

Let's get to work.
Who'd like to fetch some water for the pump?
Don't stop until the water gets cold.
Oh. Hello again. Can I help you?

Eh… My… mom said
to give this to Granny.

Satsuki, I acted just
like that when I was a boy.

I hate boys, but I'm glad he brought
this cake you made.

Dad, this house is too old.
I think it's gonna fall down.

That would be a problem,
since we just moved in here.

Everybody, try laughing.
Then whatever scares you will go away.
BOTH: One, two, one, two, one, two.

That's it. Stamp that dirt out, girls.
That's it.

GIRLS: Hi, Granny!
KUSAKABE: Good afternoon!

Hello there, Kusakabes!
Where are you headed?
We're going to visit Mom at the hospital!
Tell her I hope she's feeling better.

- Mommy!
- Hi, Mei. It's so good to see you.

Daddy kept getting lost
the whole way here, Mom.
Hey, Mom, Dad's talking to the doctor.
I am so happy to see you girls.

So how do you like it in the country?
Have you settled in?
Mommy, do you like haunted houses?

Of course. I'll have to get better soon
so I can meet some ghosts.

Mei was worried that you wouldn't
come home if the house was haunted.

- Do you two like it?
- Yeah!

Oh, what cute hair.
Did you do this, Satsuki?

- Mmm-hmm.
- It's very nice, huh, Mei?

- What a lucky girl you are.
- Yeah, but she yells at me a lot.

- That's because you never sit still.
- Let's see your hair, Satsuki.
You sure it isn't a little too short?
No, I like it this way, Mom.

- Brush my hair! Brush mine!
- Wait your turn, Mei.

It's exactly like my hair
when I was your age.

Do you think it will
start cooperating soon?
I want it to look like yours.
You and I are a lot alike, Satsuki.

- Mom looked great, didn't she, Dad?
- Yeah, she did,

her doctor said she should be able
to come home real soon.

- So she'll be home tomorrow?
- There she goes again with tomorrow.
Well, I think tomorrow might be a bit soon.

Mom said she was gonna sleep
in my bed with me.

I thought you weren't scared
to sleep alone.
She's the one who's scared, not me.

- Sorry. I overslept again.
- I have to take lunch to school today.
Oh, no. I forgot all about that.

That's okay. I'm making
lunch for everyone.

- Hey, it's burning!
- I'm coming. Just a minute.
I'm making this one for you, Mei.

There you go. Wrap it up
so it stays fresh. Okay?
So, you made a friend already, did you?

- Satsuki, time for school!
- She's walking to school with me.
Do I look like a big girl?
You do. Are you going somewhere?
I'm just off to run some errands.
Dad, you be the flower shop. Okay?
Who are you? A great big soot gremlin?
Is that what your name is? Totoro?
Yep, that's your name, all right.

- Wow. Is it that late already?
- Where's Mei?

- Did she like her lunch box?
- We haven't eaten.

She's running around
with that lunch box.

What do you think you're doing,
sleeping out here?

You mean a troll,
like the one in your storybook?
Mmm-hmm. But he called hisself Totoro.
He was furry, with a great big mouth.
There was a little one, and a bigger one,
and a huge one that kept falling asleep.

Wow. This would make
a great secret hiding place.

Dad, Mei said that
she saw a Totoro here.

revisions.text: Mmm-hmm. But he called himself Totoro.

subtitles.text: Last time the tunnel led to Totoro's tree.
I didn't see another path to take.

revisions.note: Corrected 'HEAVY' to 'HEAVILY' for proper adverb usage.

subtitles.text: Quit laughing! I really did see Totoro!

Satsuki and I believe you,
and I bet you I know what happened.

You must have met one of
the spirits of the forest.
That means you're a very lucky girl.
But you can't always see the spirits.

You can only see them
when they want you to.

Let's give the forest
spirits a proper greeting.

- A greeting?
- Of course. Come on. Let's go.
Dad, there's that big camphor tree.

SATSUKI: That's Totoro's tree?
MEI: Yeah!
Come on, Dad! Can't you go any faster?

- Are you sure it was here?
- Mmm-hmm.
Dad, she says the hole disappeared.

Then right now, the forest
spirits don't want to be seen.
But, Dad, I want to see them, too.
Maybe you will, if you're lucky.
It's been around since long ago,

back in the time when trees
and people used to be friends.

When I saw this tree, I knew this would
be a good place for our family to live.

And I think it will make
your mother feel right at home.

So, let's give this tree a nice
greeting and go eat our lunch.

Then I want to write a letter to Mom
and tell her all about this.

Thank you for watching over Mei
and making us feel so welcome here.
ALL: Please continue to look after us.

- Last one home's a rotten egg!
- Hey, no fair!

SATSUKI: Dear Mom:
We had an exciting day today.
Mei met a big forest spirit called Totoro.

I hope I'll be able
to see him someday, too.

WOMAN: Kanta, hurry up,
or you'll be late for school.

- Granny, what's wrong?
- I don't know.

She said she wouldn't stop crying
unless I brought her to you.
She said that? Mei, be reasonable.
You know you had to stay with Granny.

I have school and Dad's
teaching at the university.

I only have two more hours
of school to go.

Can't you just stay
with Granny until then?

She's been so good up till now.
Weren't you, Mei?

Well, I'll talk to my teacher
and see what we can do.
Satsuki's mom is in the hospital,

so her sister Mei is going
to stay with us today, okay, class?

- Let's make her feel welcome.
- ALL: Yes, ma'am.

- What are you drawing, Mei?
- It's a big Totoro.
Shh. Mei, try to keep quiet, will you?
I have to skip our club meeting, Michiko.
Hurry up, Mei. It's gonna rain.

- Ah! Here it comes!
- Here it comes!
I didn't even cry. That's good, huh?
Yeah, but we're not home yet.

We'd like to share your roof
for a while, sir, if you don't mind.

- That was lucky, wasn't it?
- Mmm-hmm.

- MEI: This umbrella has holes.
- Hmm.

- I better take it to him.
- I wanna go, too.

revisions.text:
- I'd better take it to him.
- I wanna go, too.
revisions.note: Changed 'I better' to 'I'd better' for grammatical correctness.

subtitles.text:
What kind of fool forgets his umbrella
at school when it's raining?

- Hey!
- I bet you were swinging it around
Oh, Satsuki and Mei. Come in. Granny!
Sorry we couldn't keep her happy.

Oh. I wanted to give back this
umbrella that Kanta lent us.

What? He lent you this?
Oh, my. Look how beat up it is.

Mei was walking home
from school with me.

So, it was really nice of him
to help us out. Tell him thank you.

- He must have been drenched.
- That's okay.

Kanta is such a dirty kid.
He can always use a good drenching.

Are you off to meet
your dad at the bus stop?

- SATSUKI: Yes, he forgot his umbrella.
- Such good kids.

- Who was at the door, Kanta?
- Beats me.
Satsuki, Dad wasn't on the bus.
He'll be on the next one for sure.

Do you want to go back
to Granny's and wait there?
What is it, Mei? Are you sleepy?

I knew this would happen.
Don't you want to wait at Granny's?
It's okay, Mei. He'll be here soon.
That bus sure is late, huh?
Hold it over your head. Like this.

Well, Mei, so much for
bringing Dad his umbrella.

- Hi. Sorry I'm late.
- WOMAN: Drive on.

My train was delayed,
so I had to wait for the next bus.
Totoro came back, Dad! I saw him!
We saw a cat bus, too!

- It was great!
- It was great!
I saw Totoro! I saw him!

- Hey, Dad, pick us up.
- Come on, Dad.

We had such a weird,
mysterious, spectacular day.
Totoro gave us a wonderful present.
It was wrapped in a bamboo leaf
and tied with a ribbon made of grass.
As soon as we got home, we opened it.

"We wanted to grow a beautiful
forest with the acorns,

“ so we planted them
in your garden out back, but…”

SATSUKI: … they just won't grow.
Mei watches them
it's starting to make her crabby.
Here's a picture of Mei as a crab.

and I can't wait to play
outside with you.
Please get well soon. Love, Satsuki.

- Lights out, girls.
- MEI: Wait, Dad.

Do you think that
they'll sprout tomorrow?

You should ask the Totoro.
He would know.

revisions.text:
“We wanted to grow a beautiful
forest with the acorns,
revisions.note: Changed the opening straight quote to a left double quote.

revisions.text:
so we planted them
in your garden out back, but…”
revisions.note: Removed the extra opening quote on the continuation subtitle and kept the closing right double quote.

subtitles.text: That's where we planted the acorns.
GIRLS: Yay! We did it! We did it!

SATSUKI: I thought it was a dream!
MEI: But it wasn't!

BOTH: I thought it was a dream!
We did it!

Wow, Granny. Your garden
is just like a market.

We've picked enough,
haven't we? Let's take a rest.

I'm glad you like it.
They're very good for you.

They've soaked up lots
of vitamins and sunshine.

- Would they help Mom get better?
- Of course they would.

If your mom eats my vegetables,
I bet she'd get well right away.

That's great, Granny.
Mom's coming home on Saturday.
She's going to sleep with me in my bed.

Oh, good! She's finally
leaving the hospital, is she?

No. The doctor wants her to get used to
the new house a little at a time.

- She goes back on Monday.
- Is that so?

We'll feed her lots of
my vegetables while she's here.

I'll give her the corn that I picked.
That will make her better.

- I bet that will do her wonders.
- Yep.

Here. The postman
left us your telegram.

What should I do with it, Granny?
Dad won't be home till evening.
Better open it. Might be an emergency.
Something must have happened to her.

We have to get to the hospital
right away, Granny.

Okay. Calm down. First, we need to get
in touch with your father.

I know his phone number at the
university, but we don't have a phone.

Kanta, take Satsuki to your uncle's
house and let her use the phone.
Mei, you need to stay here with me!
Mei, go back and stay with Granny!

Hello? I'd like to place
a long-distance call, please.
It's to my dad at Tokyo 31-13-82.
Now, that's a nice girl, eh, Kanta?

Hello? I need to speak to my dad
in the archaeology department.

I mean, I need to speak
to Mr. Kusakabe.
Hello, Dad? Is that you? It's Satsuki.

- Satsuki, what's wrong?
- We got a telegram.

You're supposed to call Mom's
doctor at the hospital.
Okay. I'll call them right away.

Did something happen to her, Dad?
Do you think she's okay?

I'll call the hospital, and then
I'll call you right back. Okay?

- Yeah.
- Just wait by the phone.

- Mmm-hmm.
- I need to hang up now. Okay?

Excuse me, is it okay if I wait here?
My dad says he's going to call me back.
Of course. Make yourself at home.
Back off! Don't touch my corn!

Too bad! She needs it
to make her better.

Mei, the doctor said
that Mom's not doing well.

So she doesn't get
to come home this weekend.

What if she came home early,
and it made her even worse?
Mei, we just have to wait a little longer.

- No!
- You want her to die, Mei?

- Is that what you want?
- No!
You're such a baby! Just grow up!

Come on, girls.
Let's put the laundry away.
Oh, don't be sad. Granny's here to help.

Don't worry. Your father is
going to stop by the hospital.

The doctors say your mom
just has a cold.
She should be home next Saturday.
This is just like last time.

They said Mom just had a little cold,
she'd be home in a few days.
Granny, what will we do if she dies?

How could she ever leave
such sweet girls like you?

I'll stay here until your father
comes home. Okay? Okay?

- She wasn't at the bus stop?
- No.

Do you know anywhere else
she might have gone?

I yelled at her this morning.
She wanted Mom to come home.

Granny, I bet she went to see Mom
at the hospital!

You think she'd try
to walk to the hospital?

Why, that's a three-hour walk
even for grown-ups.

Kanta! Hurry! Run on home
and get your father!

Mei's disappeared, and we need
all the help we can get!

I shouldn't have yelled at her.
This is all my fault.

- Yeah?
- I'm looking for my sister.

Did you happen to see
a little girl pass this way?
Let's see. Uh, a little girl?

Mmm, I don't think so.
I would have noticed.

MAN: Are you sure that this
is the way she came?

- Oh! Are you crazy?
- I'm looking for my little sister.
Have you seen a girl walking this way?

I think she's trying to walk to the
hospital. She's only four years old.
Oh. I didn't see her. Did you?
No. We just came from the hospital,

and there was no sign of
a little girl on the road.

- Wait. Where'd you say you're from?
- From Matsugo.
A four-year-old couldn't walk this far.
MAN: Good luck. I hope you find her.

- I guess you didn't, either.
- Mmm-mmm.

My dad's got a bunch of
people looking for her.

I could ride my bike
to the hospital for you.

- You should go back home.
- Check all around.

I'm sure that Mei got lost
on her way to the hospital, okay?

A little while ago,
they found a sandal in the pond.

They don't know for sure
it's Mei's sandal!
MAN: We'll check the irrigation ditch.

It's much deeper over there.
You should try there first!

I was positively certain
that sandal belonged to Mei.
All right. Granny just got carried away.

All right everyone, I know you've spent
a lot of time here already,
but we still need your help.
Let me in to see Totoro. Mei's lost.

It's getting dark,
and she's out there all alone.

Please let me in to see him.
It's an emergency.

Totoro, Mei's lost. I looked everywhere
for her, but I couldn't find her.
Please help me. I have to find her.

I'm sure she's scared
half to death by now.
I don't know where else to turn.
No one else can see it, can they?

Were you trying to take your
corn to Mom at the hospital?
You're going to take us to the hospital?

I'm sorry. I don't know why
the hospital sent a telegram.

I hope it didn't upset the girls too much.
I've caused them enough grief already.

When they see you're all right,
they'll be fine.

We'll just wait a little
longer for a weekend together.
Don't worry. We've made it this far.

The girls act so strong, but I think
it's been harder than they let on.

- She tries to act so adult.
- That's true.
I can't wait until I get better.

When I get home, I'm going
to spoil those girls rotten.
I'm going to hurry up and get well!

I thought I saw Satsuki and Mei
smiling at us from up in that tree.
It sounds crazy, but maybe you did.
♪ Follow them and you will see ♪
♪ It may seem like a dream ♪
♪ Things are not what they seem ♪
♪ For such a very, very long time ♪
♪ There you'll be with Totoro, Totoro ♪

♪ You only see him
When you're very young ♪
♪ Watch him play in the rain ♪
♪ He plays a magic flute in the sky ♪

♪ You only see him
When you're very young ♪
